 {"id":"asupersync-59c.5.2","title":"Experiment: graded/quantitative types for obligations and budgets","description":"# Experiment: Graded/Quantitative Types

## Purpose
Explore an opt-in type layer where obligations and budgets carry resource annotations.

## Goal
Make “no obligation leaks” a type error for code using the graded surface.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A small prototype encodes:
  - `Obligation<K, 1>` reserve/commit/abort
  - a typing judgment sketch (documented)
- Demonstrate with a toy API that leaking obligations is untypeable.

 {"id":"asupersync-59c.5.3","title":"Tooling: VASS/WSTS projection for obligation marking analysis","description":"# VASS/WSTS Obligation Marking Analysis Tool

## Purpose
Project obligation registry behavior into a vector-addition system (token counts per obligation kind/region) to enable:
- fast trace checks
- bounded coverability-style analyses

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tool can consume traces and output marking evolution.
- Detects leak states.

 {"id":"asupersync-59c.6","title":"Design Contract: Advanced Mathematical Lenses (tracking)","description":"# Design Contract: Advanced Mathematical Lenses (Tracking)

## Purpose
The design documents include several advanced mathematical lenses that are part of the long-term contract. Phase 0 does not need to implement them explicitly, but **must not preclude them**.

This feature tracks those lenses as explicit “don’t break this later” constraints and, where useful, as concrete experiments.

## Lenses to Track
- Event structures / HDA / directed topology (tracked separately in Phase 5+)
- Quantitative/graded types (tracked separately in Phase 5+)
- Polynomial functors for compositional dynamics
- Dialectica view of two-phase effects (forward value + backward obligation)
- Guarded recursion for actors/leases
- Cancellation as a quantitative game (already partially encoded via bounded mask/checkpoint budgets)
- Lyapunov-guided scheduling governor (optional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each lens has:
  - a concise statement of “what it would buy us”
  - a concrete preservation constraint on the runtime semantics
  - a test/tooling hook where applicable

-{"id":"asupersync-59c.6.1","title":"Track polynomial functor laws for compositional dynamics (plan compatibility)","description":"# Polynomial Functor Lens (Tracking)

## Purpose
The design notes that tasks/regions/combinators can be viewed through polynomial functors to derive composition laws categorically.

We do not need to implement category theory in Phase 0, but we must:
- preserve associativity/unit laws for join/race up to observational equivalence
- avoid ad-hoc semantics that break lawful rewrites

## Deliverables
- Document the specific join/race/timeout laws we commit to.
- Identify which laws are conditional on policy (commutativity, distributivity).

## Acceptance Criteria
- A “law sheet” exists as part of this bead (so we don’t need the original docs).
- Tests exist that validate committed laws for Phase 0 combinators.

 {"id":"asupersync-59c.6.2","title":"Track Dialectica-style two-phase effects contract (reserve/commit obligations)","description":"# Dialectica Lens for Two-Phase Effects (Tracking)

## Purpose
Two-phase effects can be viewed as Dialectica morphisms: forward value + backward obligation.

Pragmatically, this means we must preserve:
- reserve is cancel-safe (no effect committed)
- commit is linear and must either happen or abort
- dropping a permit has defined semantics (abort/nack)

## Deliverables
- Explicit contract for permit/ack/lease/IoOp behavior on drop, cancel, region close.
- Tests that encode the contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No primitive can “half commit” an effect.
- Lab oracles detect any obligation leak.

 {"id":"asupersync-59c.6.3","title":"Track guarded recursion lens for actors/leases (time-indexed behavior)","description":"# Guarded Recursion Lens (Tracking)

## Purpose
The design suggests modeling long-lived behaviors (actors, leases) using guarded recursion / “later” modality:
- makes coinductive reasoning and time-indexed lease semantics principled

## Practical Preservation Constraints
- Actor behavior should be representable as a state machine that evolves per message/time step.
- Leases must have explicit renewal/expiry semantics tied to time.

## Deliverables
- Document how actor/lease APIs map to guarded recursion intuition.
- Identify which runtime invariants depend on time-indexing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Captures the guarded-recursion/"later" modality lens as a concrete design note tied to actor and lease APIs.
- Identifies at least one practical payoff (e.g., time-indexed lease renewal reasoning or actor restart protocol invariants).
- Produces a checklist of constraints to preserve in earlier phases so this lens remains valid.

 {"id":"asupersync-59c.6.4","title":"Experiment: Lyapunov-guided scheduling governor","description":"# Experiment: Lyapunov-Guided Scheduling Governor

## Purpose
The design includes an optional but high-leverage idea:
- define a potential function V(Σ) over runtime state
- choose scheduling actions that decrease V (or prioritize cancel-lane decrease)

This provides a principled argument that cancellation converges to quiescence.

## Deliverables
- Propose one or two candidate V(Σ) definitions using:
  - live child count
  - outstanding obligations (weighted by age/priority)
  - remaining finalizers
  - deadline slack
- Implement a prototype governor in lab mode.

## Success Metrics
- Reduces cancellation tail latency vs naive scheduler on stress tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Defines a concrete potential function V(Σ) candidate and an evaluation strategy.
- Specifies which scheduler/governor decisions are allowed to optimize V(Σ) without violating cancellation/quiescence invariants.
- Provides at least one deterministic experiment in the lab runtime demonstrating improved drain behavior or reduced starvation.
